title: TCP Session Handler
description:
Implement TCP session handler for HOPR network.

FUNCTIONALITY:
Create TCP sessions that route through HOPR network.
Support 0-3 hop routes.

TCP HANDLER API:
pub struct TcpSessionHandler;

impl TcpSessionHandler {
    pub async fn create(
        client: &HoprdClient,
        route: Vec<String>,
        config: &SessionConfig
    ) -> Result<TcpSession, SessionError>
}

pub struct TcpSession {
    id: String,
    // Connection details
}

impl TcpSession {
    pub async fn send(&mut self, data: &[u8]) -> Result<usize, SessionError>
    pub async fn recv(&mut self, buf: &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize, SessionError>
    pub async fn close(self) -> Result<(), SessionError>
}

ERROR HANDLING:
- Connection timeout
- Route establishment failure
- Send/receive errors
- Network disconnection

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ Create TCP session through HOPR network
✓ Send data through session
✓ Receive data from session
✓ Handle connection errors gracefully
✓ Integration test with 0-hop session
✓ Proper cleanup on close

title: Latency Benchmark
description:
Implement latency benchmark with ping-pong pattern.

LATENCY BENCHMARK:
Measure round-trip time (RTT) by sending small packets and measuring response time.

PATTERN:
1. Send packet with timestamp
2. Wait for response
3. Calculate RTT = receive_time - send_time
4. Repeat N times (configurable iterations)
5. Calculate percentiles

METRICS TO COLLECT:
- Individual RTT samples (all iterations)
- Statistical analysis:
  - min: minimum RTT
  - max: maximum RTT
  - mean: average RTT
  - median: 50th percentile
  - p95: 95th percentile
  - p99: 99th percentile
  - stddev: standard deviation

IMPLEMENTATION:
Use hdrhistogram crate for efficient percentile calculation.

pub struct LatencyBenchmark;

impl LatencyBenchmark {
    pub async fn run(
        session_id: &str,
        iterations: u32,
        session_manager: &SessionManager
    ) -> Result<LatencyMetrics, BenchmarkError>
}

pub struct LatencyMetrics {
    pub samples: Vec<Duration>,
    pub min: Duration,
    pub max: Duration,
    pub mean: Duration,
    pub median: Duration,
    pub p95: Duration,
    pub p99: Duration,
    pub stddev: f64,
}

PACKET FORMAT:
Small packets (64 bytes):
- 8 bytes: sequence number (u64)
- 8 bytes: timestamp (u64, nanoseconds)
- 48 bytes: padding

TIMEOUT:
Individual packet timeout: 5 seconds
Total benchmark timeout: iterations * 10 seconds

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ Send N packets and measure RTT
✓ Use hdrhistogram for percentiles
✓ Calculate min, max, mean, median, p95, p99
✓ Configurable iterations from config
✓ Results displayed in TUI
✓ Handle timeouts gracefully


DOCUMENTATION REQUIREMENT:
Upon completion, you MUST update all relevant documentation:
- README.md: Update if user-facing behavior or installation changes
- AGENTS.md: Update if new modules, APIs, or architecture changes
- Architecture doc: Update if system design or data flow changes
Include these updates in the same commit/PR as the task completion.
close_reason: Completed latency benchmark infrastructure: HDR Histogram integration for percentiles (min/max/mean/median/p95/p99/stddev), comprehensive LatencyMetrics tests, and benchmark view displaying all metrics. Actual ping-pong measurement and timeout handling require HOPR protocol implementation (placeholder in place).

title: Keyboard Shortcuts
description:
Implement all keyboard shortcuts for TUI.

KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS:

Global (all views):
- Tab: Switch to next view (cycle: Sessions → Channels → Benchmark → Config → Help → Sessions)
- Shift+Tab: Switch to previous view
- Q: Quit application (with confirmation if sessions active)
- R: Refresh current view data
- ?: Toggle help view

Session List View:
- N: Create new session (open dialog)
- C: Close selected session
- D: Show debug view for selected session
- L: Show logs filtered by selected session
- B: Run benchmark on selected session
- ↑/↓: Navigate session list
- Enter: Show session details (expand details panel)

Session Debug View:
- ESC: Return to session list
- ↑/↓: Scroll content

Channel View:
- ↑/↓: Navigate channel list
- F: Change node filter (cycle through nodes)

Config View:
- ↑/↓: Scroll configuration

Help View:
- ESC or ?: Close help view
- ↑/↓: Scroll help content

Session Creation Dialog:
- Tab/Shift+Tab: Move between fields
- ↑/↓: Change selection in field
- Enter: Submit (create session)
- ESC: Cancel and close dialog

Benchmark View:
- ↑/↓: Navigate results
- E: Export results to JSON

IMPLEMENTATION:
Use crossterm KeyEvent handling.
Match on KeyCode and KeyModifiers.
Dispatch to appropriate handler based on current view.

ERROR HANDLING:
- Ignore invalid key combinations
- Show status message for unimplemented shortcuts
- Handle Ctrl+C for emergency exit

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ All shortcuts work as documented
✓ Tab navigation cycles views
✓ View-specific shortcuts active only in that view
✓ ESC always goes back/cancels
✓ Q quits with confirmation if needed
✓ Ctrl+C emergency exit works

title: Session Debug View
description:
Implement detailed session debug view.

VIEW LAYOUT:
Press 'D' on selected session to open this view.

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Session Debug: abc-123                   [ESC] Back│
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ State Transition History:                          │
│  14:23:15.123 [Initializing ] → Session created   │
│  14:23:15.456 [Establishing] → Route building     │
│  14:23:16.234 [Establishing] → Channel validated  │
│  14:23:17.123 [Active      ] → Session established│
│  14:25:42.567 [Degraded    ] → High latency >100ms│
│                                                     │
│ Frame Buffer Status:                               │
│  Capacity: 8192 bytes                              │
│  Current:  2341 bytes (28% full)                   │
│  Incomplete frames: 2                              │
│    - Frame #127: 456 bytes, waiting 234ms         │
│    - Frame #128: 123 bytes, waiting  89ms         │
│  Timeout threshold: 800ms                          │
│                                                     │
│ Route Details:                                     │
│  Entry:  entry-node (16Uiu2HAm...)                │
│    ↓ Channel: 0x8f3a... [OPEN] Balance: 0.5 HOPR │
│  Relay1: relay-node-1 (16Uiu2HAm...)              │
│    ↓ Channel: 0x2b4c... [OPEN] Balance: 1.2 HOPR │
│  Exit:   exit-node (16Uiu2HAm...)                 │
│                                                     │
│ Recent Events (last 10):                           │
│  14:26:01.234 PKT_SENT     Seq=1250 Size=512      │
│  14:26:01.245 PKT_RECEIVED Seq=1249 Size=480      │
│  14:26:01.298 TIMEOUT_WARN Frame #125 >750ms      │
│  14:26:01.302 FRAME_COMPLETE Frame #125 4096 bytes│
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

SECTIONS:
1. State Transition History (scrollable, newest first)
2. Frame Buffer Status (current state)
3. Route Details (topology with channel info)
4. Recent Events (last 10, scrollable)

INTERACTION:
- ESC: return to session list
- Up/Down: scroll sections
- Auto-update every 500ms

COLOR CODING:
- State transitions: green (positive), yellow (warning), red (error)
- Buffer usage: green (<50%), yellow (50-80%), red (>80%)
- Timeouts: yellow (>50% threshold), red (>80% threshold)
- Events: white (normal), yellow (warning), red (error)

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ Show state transition history

title: Debug Info Collection
description:
Implement session debug information collection.

TRACKED DEBUG INFO:

1. State Transition History:
   - Record every state change with timestamp
   - Include reason for transition
   - Preserve full history (no limit)

2. Frame Buffer Monitoring:
   - Track buffer capacity and current usage
   - List incomplete frames (frame_id, bytes, waiting_time)
   - Monitor timeout threshold (HOPR_SESSION_FRAME_TIMEOUT_MS)
   - Alert when frames approach timeout (>80% of threshold)

3. Session Event Logging:
   - PacketSent (seq number, size)
   - PacketReceived (seq number, size)
   - TimeoutWarning (frame_id, waiting time)
   - FrameComplete (frame_id, total size)
   - Error events with message

4. Route Debug Info:
   - Node details (name, peer_id)
   - Channel info between hops (id, status, balance)
   - Network latency per hop (if measurable)

API:
impl SessionManager {
    pub fn get_debug_info(&self, session_id: &str) -> Option<&SessionDebugInfo>
    pub fn record_state_transition(&mut self, session_id: &str, transition: StateTransition)
    pub fn record_event(&mut self, session_id: &str, event: SessionEvent)
    pub fn update_buffer_status(&mut self, session_id: &str, status: BufferStatus)
}

STORAGE:
Store in SessionDebugInfo within Session struct.
Use VecDeque for events (limit to last 1000).
Vec for state history (unlimited).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ State transition history tracking
✓ Frame buffer status monitoring
✓ Session event logging (1000 recent)
✓ Route debug info with channels/balances
✓ Debug info accessible via SessionManager

title: Benchmark Runner
description:
Implement benchmark runner for performance testing.

RUNNER API:
pub struct BenchmarkRunner {
    session_manager: Arc<Mutex<SessionManager>>,
    config: BenchmarkConfig,
}

impl BenchmarkRunner {
    pub fn new(
        session_manager: Arc<Mutex<SessionManager>>,
        config: BenchmarkConfig
    ) -> Self

    pub async fn run_benchmark(
        &self,
        session_id: &str,
        benchmark_type: BenchmarkType
    ) -> Result<BenchmarkResult, BenchmarkError>

    pub async fn cancel(&self) -> Result<(), BenchmarkError>
}

pub enum BenchmarkType {
    Latency { iterations: u32 },
    Throughput { payload_size: usize },
    Stress { concurrent_sessions: u32, duration: Duration },
}

pub struct BenchmarkResult {
    pub session_id: String,
    pub hop_count: HopCount,
    pub protocol: Protocol,
    pub benchmark_type: BenchmarkType,
    pub started_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub duration: Duration,
    pub success: bool,
    pub error: Option<String>,
    // Type-specific results
    pub latency_metrics: Option<LatencyMetrics>,
    pub throughput_metrics: Option<ThroughputMetrics>,
    pub stress_metrics: Option<StressMetrics>,
}

FEATURES:
- Run benchmarks on existing sessions
- Support cancellation (graceful stop)
- Collect detailed metrics
- Return structured results
- Handle errors gracefully

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Async implementation with Tokio
- Use tokio::select! for cancellation
- Spawn tasks for concurrent operations
- Clean up resources on error

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ BenchmarkRunner struct with async methods
✓ Run benchmark on existing session
✓ Return BenchmarkResult with metrics
✓ Support cancellation
✓ Proper error handling

title: Implement CLI Parsing
description:
Implement CLI argument parsing with clap.

REQUIRED FLAGS:
- --config / -c <path> : Config file path (default: config.toml)
- --log-level <level> : Log level (trace/debug/info/warn/error, default: info)
- --quick-session : Non-interactive session creation mode
- --protocol <tcp|udp> : Protocol for quick session (default: tcp)
- --hops <0-3> : Number of hops (default: 0)
- --route <nodes> : Comma-separated node names
- --benchmark : Run in benchmark mode
- --iterations <n> : Benchmark iterations (default: 100)

COMMANDS:
- hose --version : Show version
- hose --help : Show help text

IMPLEMENTATION:
Use clap derive macros for clean CLI definition.
Invalid arguments must show helpful error messages.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

✓ hose --help displays comprehensive help
✓ All flags parse correctly

title: Configuration Management
description:
Implement TOML configuration parsing and validation.

CONFIG STRUCTURE:
[hoprd]
[[hoprd.nodes]]
name = string (node identifier)
api_endpoint = string (URL)
api_token = string (optional, Bearer auth)
role = "Entry" | "Relay" | "Exit"
peer_id = string (optional, HOPRd peer ID)

[sessions]
default_protocol = "TCP" | "UDP"
timeout_ms = u64 (must be >= 1000)
max_retries = u32
buffer_size = usize

[benchmark]
iterations = u32 (must be > 0)
payload_sizes = array of usize
concurrency = u32

[tui]
refresh_rate_ms = u64
theme = "dark" | "light"
log_level = string

VALIDATION RULES:
- At least one node must be configured
- timeout_ms >= 1000 (minimum 1 second)
- benchmark iterations > 0
- At least one Entry and one Exit node
- Valid URLs for api_endpoint

ENVIRONMENT OVERRIDE:
Support HOSE_* prefix (e.g., HOSE_LOG_LEVEL)

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ Parse valid config.toml successfully
✓ Validation catches all invalid values
✓ Environment variables override config
✓ Clear, actionable error messages
✓ Unit tests for validation logic pass

title: API Response Models
description:

REQUIRED MODELS:

NodeInfo:
- id: String (peer ID)
- address: String (network address)
- version: String
- peers: Vec<String> (connected peer IDs)

Channel:
- id: String (channel ID)
- source: String (peer ID)
- destination: String (peer ID)
- status: ChannelStatus enum (Open, Closed, PendingToClose)
- balance: f64 (HOPR tokens)

SessionResponse:
- id: String (session ID)
- status: SessionStatus enum
- route: Vec<String> (peer IDs)

Use serde for JSON deserialization.

title: Route Validation
description:
Implement route validation before session creation.

VALIDATION CHECKS:

1. Node Configuration:
   - All nodes in route exist in config
   - Entry node has role=Entry
   - Exit node has role=Exit
   - Intermediate nodes have role=Relay
   - Hop count matches route length

2. Channel Availability:
   - Query HOPRd for channels between each hop
   - Verify channel status is OPEN (not Closed or PendingToClose)
   - Check sufficient balance for message forwarding
   - Validate peer IDs match expected nodes

3. Route Topology:
   - Entry → Relay(s) → Exit
   - No duplicate nodes in route
   - Route length matches hop count (0-3)

VALIDATION API:
pub struct RouteValidator;

impl RouteValidator {
    pub async fn validate_route(
        route: &[String],
        hop_count: HopCount,
        config: &HoseConfig,
        client: &HoprdClient
    ) -> Result<RouteValidation, ValidationError>
}

pub struct RouteValidation {
    pub valid: bool,
    pub errors: Vec<String>,
    pub warnings: Vec<String>,
    pub channel_details: Vec<ChannelInfo>,
}

ERROR MESSAGES:
Must be clear and actionable:
- "Node 'relay1' not found in config"
- "No OPEN channel from entry-node to relay1"
- "Route length (3) doesn't match hop count (2)"

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ Check all nodes exist in config
✓ Verify channels exist between hops
✓ Check channel states are OPEN
✓ Clear error messages if invalid
✓ Unit tests for validation logic

title: HOPRd HTTP Client
description:
Implement HTTP client for HOPRd REST API.

CLIENT FEATURES:
- reqwest-based async HTTP client
- Bearer token authentication (optional per node)
- Retry logic with exponential backoff (max 3 retries)
- Configurable connection timeout
- Base URL per node from config
- JSON request/response handling

ERROR HANDLING:
Create ApiError enum covering:
- Network errors (connection refused, timeout)
- HTTP errors (4xx, 5xx)
- Deserialization errors
- Authentication failures

RETRY STRATEGY:
- Retry on 5xx errors and network failures
- Exponential backoff: 100ms, 200ms, 400ms
- No retry on 4xx errors (client errors)

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ HoprdClient struct with constructor
✓ Auth token support (Bearer in header)
✓ Retry logic implemented and tested
✓ Connection timeout configurable
✓ Unit tests with mockito pass

title: Implement API Endpoints
description:
Implement HOPRd API endpoint methods.

REQUIRED ENDPOINTS:

Node Information:
- GET /api/v3/node/info → NodeInfo
- GET /api/v3/node/peers → Vec<PeerInfo>

Channel Management:
- GET /api/v3/channels → Vec<Channel>

title: Session List View
description:

VIEW LAYOUT:

title: Error Handling
description:
Improve error messages and error handling.

ERROR CATEGORIES:

1. Configuration Errors:
   - Missing config file → "Config file not found at 'config.toml'. Create it with 'cp config.example.toml config.toml'"
   - Invalid TOML → "Failed to parse config: invalid TOML syntax at line 15"
   - Missing nodes → "No nodes configured in [hoprd.nodes]. Add at least one Entry and one Exit node."
   - Invalid timeout → "Session timeout must be at least 1000ms, got 500ms"

2. Network Errors:
   - Connection refused → "Cannot connect to HOPRd at http://localhost:3001. Is the node running?"
   - Timeout → "Request to HOPRd timed out after 10s. Check network connection."
   - DNS failure → "Cannot resolve hostname 'hoprd.example.com'. Check DNS settings."

3. API Errors:
   - 401 Unauthorized → "Authentication failed. Check api_token in config for node 'entry-node'."
   - 404 Not Found → "Endpoint not found. Is HOPRd version compatible?"
   - 500 Server Error → "HOPRd internal error. Check node logs."

4. Session Errors:
   - Invalid route → "Invalid route: node 'relay1' not found in config. Available nodes: entry-node, relay2, exit-node"
   - No channels → "No OPEN channel from 'entry-node' to 'relay1'. Check channel status with 'hoprd-admin channels list'."
   - Session timeout → "Session establishment timed out after 5s. Check node connectivity."

5. Benchmark Errors:
   - Session not found → "Cannot run benchmark: session 'abc-123' not found. Has it been closed?"
   - Benchmark timeout → "Benchmark timed out after 60s. Check network stability."

ERROR HANDLING PRINCIPLES:
- Every error includes context (what failed, why, how to fix)
- Suggest corrective actions
- Include relevant values (timeout used, expected range)
- Never panic in normal operation
- Use Result types consistently
- Log errors with appropriate levels

IMPLEMENTATION:
Use thiserror for error types:

#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
pub enum SessionError {
    #[error("Node '{node}' not found in config. Available nodes: {available}")]
    NodeNotFound { node: String, available: String },

    #[error("No OPEN channel from '{from}' to '{to}'. Run 'hoprd-admin channels list' to check status.")]
    NoChannel { from: String, to: String },

    // ... more variants
}

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ All errors are user-friendly
✓ Error messages suggest corrective actions
✓ Include context (what, why, how to fix)
✓ No panics in normal operation
✓ Graceful degradation on API failures
✓ Log errors appropriately

title: Session Manager
description:
Implement SessionManager for lifecycle management.

MANAGER API:
pub struct SessionManager {
    sessions: HashMap<String, Session>,
    hoprd_client: Arc<HoprdClient>,
    config: SessionConfig,
}

impl SessionManager {
    pub fn new(client: Arc<HoprdClient>, config: SessionConfig) -> Self

    pub async fn create_session(
        &mut self,
        protocol: Protocol,
        hop_count: HopCount,
        route: Vec<String>
    ) -> Result<String, SessionError>

    pub async fn get_session(&self, id: &str) -> Option<&Session>

    pub async fn get_session_mut(&mut self, id: &str) -> Option<&mut Session>

    pub async fn close_session(&mut self, id: &str) -> Result<(), SessionError>

    pub async fn list_sessions(&self) -> Vec<&Session>

    pub async fn update_metrics(&mut self, id: &str) -> Result<(), SessionError>
}

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Use HashMap<String, Session> for storage (key = UUID)
- Generate UUIDs with uuid crate (v4)
- Async implementation using Tokio
- Session creation spawns async task for background work
- Proper error handling with SessionError type

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ create_session() returns session ID
✓ get_session() retrieves by ID
✓ close_session() terminates gracefully
✓ list_sessions() returns all active
✓ HashMap storage with UUID keys

✓ Unit tests pass

title: Code Documentation
Session Data Types
description:
Define session-related data types.

SESSION TYPES:

HopCount enum:
- Zero (direct connection)
- One (1 relay)
- Two (2 relays)
- Three (3 relays)

SessionState enum:
- Initializing (just created)
- Establishing (building route)
- Active (sending/receiving data)
- Degraded (high latency or packet loss)
- Closed (terminated)
- Error(String) (failed with reason)

Session struct:
- id: String (UUID)
- protocol: Protocol (TCP or UDP)
- hop_count: HopCount
- route: Vec<String> (node names from config)
- state: SessionState
- created_at: DateTime<Utc>
- metrics: SessionMetrics

SessionMetrics struct:
- bytes_sent: u64
- bytes_received: u64
- packets_sent: u64
- packets_received: u64
- latency_ms: Option<f64>
- packet_loss: f64

SessionDebugInfo struct:
- state_history: Vec<StateTransition>
- buffer_status: BufferStatus
- route_details: RouteDebugInfo
- recent_events: VecDeque<SessionEvent>

StateTransition struct:
- timestamp: DateTime<Utc>
- from_state: SessionState
- to_state: SessionState
- reason: String

BufferStatus struct:
- capacity: usize
- current: usize
- incomplete_frames: Vec<IncompleteFrame>
- timeout_ms: u64

IncompleteFrame struct:
- frame_id: u64
- bytes: usize
- waiting_ms: u64

SessionEvent enum:
- PacketSent { seq: u64, size: usize }
- PacketReceived { seq: u64, size: usize }
- TimeoutWarning { frame_id: u64, waiting_ms: u64 }
- FrameComplete { frame_id: u64, size: usize }
- Error { message: String }

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ All types defined with proper fields
✓ Derive: Debug, Clone
✓ Unit tests for state transitions
✓ State machine logic is sound

Upon completion, you MUST update all relevant documentation:
- README.md: Update if user-facing behavior or installation changes

title: Session Creation Dialog
description:
Implement interactive session creation dialog.

DIALOG LAYOUT:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Create New Session                                  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                     │
│ Protocol:  [●TCP] [ UDP]                           │
│                                                     │
│ Hop Count: [ 0] [●1] [ 2] [ 3]                     │
│                                                     │
│ Route Configuration:                               │
│   Entry:  [entry-node      ▼]                     │
│   Relay1: [relay-node-1    ▼]                     │
│   Exit:   [exit-node       ▼]                     │
│                                                     │
│ Validation:                                        │
│   ✓ All nodes configured                           │
│   ✓ Channels available                             │
│                                                     │
│ [Create] [Cancel]                                  │

BEHAVIOR:
- Open with 'N' key from session list
- Tab/Shift+Tab: move between fields
- Arrow keys: change selection within field
- Enter: submit (create session)
- ESC: cancel and close

PROTOCOL SELECTION:
- Radio buttons: TCP or UDP
- Default: TCP

HOP COUNT SELECTION:
- Radio buttons: 0, 1, 2, or 3
- Updates route fields (hide/show relays based on count)
- 0 hops: Entry → Exit (no relays)
- 1 hop: Entry → Relay1 → Exit
- 2 hops: Entry → Relay1 → Relay2 → Exit
- 3 hops: Entry → Relay1 → Relay2 → Relay3 → Exit

ROUTE SELECTION:
- Dropdowns populated from config (filter by role)
- Entry dropdown: only Entry nodes
- Relay dropdowns: only Relay nodes
- Exit dropdown: only Exit nodes

VALIDATION (real-time):
- Check all required fields filled
- Validate route (query channels)
- Show validation status:
  - ✓ Green checkmark: valid
  - ✗ Red X with error message: invalid
- Disable "Create" button if invalid

ERROR MESSAGES:
- "No Entry nodes configured"
- "No Relay nodes configured"
- "No channel from entry to relay1"
- "Channel relay1→relay2 is CLOSED"

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ Press 'N' to open dialog
✓ Select protocol (TCP/UDP)
✓ Select hop count (0-3)
✓ Select nodes for route from config
✓ Route fields adapt to hop count
✓ Create button triggers session creation
✓ Cancel button closes dialog
✓ Show validation errors in real-time

title: Throughput Benchmark
description:
Implement throughput benchmark with bulk transfer.

THROUGHPUT BENCHMARK:
Measure maximum data transfer rate by sending large payloads.

PATTERN:
1. Send large payload continuously
2. Measure bytes transferred and time elapsed
3. Calculate throughput = bytes / seconds
4. Calculate packet rate = packets / seconds
5. Test with multiple payload sizes

METRICS TO COLLECT:
- Total bytes sent
- Total bytes received
- Duration of test
- Throughput (bytes per second)
- Packet rate (packets per second)
- Packet loss (if any)

IMPLEMENTATION:
pub struct ThroughputBenchmark;

impl ThroughputBenchmark {
    pub async fn run(
        session_id: &str,
        payload_size: usize,
        duration: Duration,
        session_manager: &SessionManager
    ) -> Result<ThroughputMetrics, BenchmarkError>
}

pub struct ThroughputMetrics {
    pub bytes_sent: u64,
    pub bytes_received: u64,
    pub duration: Duration,
    pub throughput_bps: f64,      // bytes per second
    pub throughput_mbps: f64,     // megabits per second
    pub packet_rate: f64,         // packets per second
    pub packet_loss_pct: f64,     // percentage
}

PAYLOAD SIZES (configurable):
- 64 bytes (tiny)
- 512 bytes (small)
- 1024 bytes (1 KB)
- 4096 bytes (4 KB)
- 8192 bytes (8 KB)
- 65536 bytes (64 KB)

TEST DURATION:
- Default: 10 seconds per payload size
- Configurable via config

SEND STRATEGY:
- Send packets as fast as possible
- Track sent/received counters
- Calculate stats at end

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ Send large payload continuously
✓ Measure transfer rate
✓ Calculate bytes/second and packets/second
✓ Configurable payload sizes from config
✓ Test multiple payload sizes
✓ Results displayed in TUI
✓ Calculate throughput in bps and Mbps

- AGENTS.md: Update if new modules, APIs, or architecture changes
- Architecture doc: Update if system design or data flow changes
Include these updates in the same commit/PR as the task completion.

Benchmark view displays throughput metrics. Placeholder runner implementation exists. Actual bulk transfer measurement requires HOPR protocol implementation.

title: Documentation
description:
Create comprehensive README and documentation.

README STRUCTURE:

# HOPR Session Debugger (hose)

## Overview
Brief description of what hose does and why it's useful.

## Features
- Interactive TUI for session debugging
- TCP/UDP session support (0-3 hops)
- Real-time metrics and diagnostics
- Session debug view (state history, buffers, events)
- Performance benchmarking
- HOPRd API integration
- Multi-node configuration

## Installation

### Prerequisites
- Rust 1.70+ and Cargo
- Running HOPRd node(s)
- HOPRd API access token(s)

### Build from Source
```bash
git clone https://github.com/hoprnet/hopr-session-debugger
cd hopr-session-debugger
cargo build --release
./target/release/hose --version
```

## Quick Start

1. Create configuration:
```bash
cp config.example.toml config.toml
# Edit config.toml with your node details
```

2. Launch hose:
```bash
hose --config config.toml
```

3. Create session:
- Press 'N' to open session creation dialog
- Select protocol, hop count, and route
- Press Enter to create

4. Debug session:
- Select session with arrow keys
- Press 'D' to view detailed debug info

## Configuration Reference

### [hoprd.nodes]
Node configuration section...

### [sessions]
Session defaults...

### [benchmark]
Benchmark settings...

(Full reference with all options, types, defaults)

## Keyboard Shortcuts

Global:
- Tab - Switch views
- Q - Quit
- R - Refresh
- ? - Help

Session View:
- N - New session
- D - Debug view
- C - Close session
- B - Benchmark

(Complete list from help view)

## Troubleshooting

### Cannot connect to HOPRd
Check node is running: 

### No channels available
Open channels: 

### Session establishment timeout
Check node connectivity and channel balances.

(More common issues and solutions)

## Architecture Overview

High-level system design diagram and component descriptions.

## API Integration

Details on HOPRd API usage, endpoints, authentication.

## Contributing

Link to CONTRIBUTING.md

## License

License information

title: Stress Test
description:
Implement concurrent session stress test.

STRESS TEST:
Test system behavior under load with many concurrent sessions.

TEST PATTERN:
1. Create N concurrent sessions
2. Send traffic through all sessions simultaneously
3. Run for duration D
4. Track success/failure rates
5. Measure resource usage
6. Report aggregate statistics

METRICS TO COLLECT:
- Total sessions created
- Successful session establishments
- Failed session establishments
- Total packets sent/received
- Average latency across all sessions
- Packet loss rate
- Memory usage
- CPU usage (if possible)

IMPLEMENTATION:
pub struct StressTest;

impl StressTest {
    pub async fn run(
        concurrent_sessions: u32,
        duration: Duration,
        session_manager: &SessionManager,
        hoprd_client: &HoprdClient,
        config: &SessionConfig
    ) -> Result<StressMetrics, BenchmarkError>
}

pub struct StressMetrics {
    pub total_sessions: u32,
    pub successful_establishments: u32,
    pub failed_establishments: u32,
    pub total_packets_sent: u64,
    pub total_packets_received: u64,
    pub average_latency_ms: f64,
    pub packet_loss_pct: f64,
    pub duration: Duration,
    pub memory_usage_mb: Option<f64>,
    pub errors: Vec<String>,
}

CONCURRENCY:
- Use Tokio tasks for each session
- Join all tasks at end
- Collect results from each task
- Aggregate statistics

CONFIGURATION:
- Default concurrent_sessions: 10
- Default duration: 60 seconds
- Configurable via benchmark config

ERROR HANDLING:
- Continue on individual session failures
- Track errors but don't abort test
- Report all errors in results

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ Create N concurrent sessions
✓ Run load for duration D
✓ Track success/failure rates
✓ Measure resource usage (memory)
✓ Report aggregate statistics
✓ Handle individual session failures gracefully

Upon completion, you MUST update all relevant documentation:
- README.md: Update if user-facing behavior or installation changes
- AGENTS.md: Update if new modules, APIs, or architecture changes
- Architecture doc: Update if system design or data flow changes
Include these updates in the same commit/PR as the task completion.

title: UDP Session Handler
description:
Implement UDP session handler for HOPR network.

FUNCTIONALITY:
Create UDP sessions that route through HOPR network.
Support 0-3 hop routes.
Handle packet loss gracefully (expected behavior for UDP).

UDP HANDLER API:
pub struct UdpSessionHandler;

impl UdpSessionHandler {
    pub async fn create(
        client: &HoprdClient,
        route: Vec<String>,
        config: &SessionConfig
    ) -> Result<UdpSession, SessionError>
}

pub struct UdpSession {
    id: String,
    // Connection details
}

impl UdpSession {
    pub async fn send_datagram(&mut self, data: &[u8]) -> Result<(), SessionError>
    pub async fn recv_datagram(&mut self, buf: &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize, SessionError>
    pub async fn close(self) -> Result<(), SessionError>
}

UDP CHARACTERISTICS:
- No guaranteed delivery (track packet loss in metrics)
- No connection state (connectionless)
- May receive datagrams out of order
- Maximum datagram size limits

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ Create UDP session through HOPR network
✓ Send datagrams through session
✓ Receive datagrams from session
✓ Handle packet loss gracefully (no panic)
✓ Integration test with 0-hop session
✓ Track packet loss statistics

title: Integration Tests
description:

TEST SCENARIOS:

1. Session Creation Tests:

5. API Integration:

title: Metrics Collection
description:
Implement metrics collection for sessions.

METRICS TO TRACK:

Real-time Metrics:
- bytes_sent: u64 (cumulative)
- bytes_received: u64 (cumulative)
- packets_sent: u64 (counter)
- packets_received: u64 (counter)
- latency_ms: Option<f64> (rolling average)
- packet_loss: f64 (percentage, 0.0-100.0)

Calculated Metrics:
- throughput_bps: bytes per second (sliding window)
- packet_rate: packets per second
- success_rate: (packets_received / packets_sent) * 100

COLLECTION STRATEGY:
- Update on every packet send/receive
- Calculate latency with timestamps (send → receive)
- Use sliding window (last 10 seconds) for throughput
- Update packet loss ratio in real-time

API:
impl SessionMetrics {
    pub fn new() -> Self
    pub fn record_sent(&mut self, bytes: usize)
    pub fn record_received(&mut self, bytes: usize, latency_ms: Option<f64>)
    pub fn calculate_packet_loss(&self) -> f64
    pub fn throughput_bps(&self) -> f64
}

impl SessionManager {
    pub async fn update_metrics(&mut self, session_id: &str) -> Result<(), SessionError>
}

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ Track bytes/packets sent and received

title: Help View
description:
Implement help view with keyboard shortcuts.

VIEW LAYOUT:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Help - Keyboard Shortcuts                          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Global:                                            │
│   Tab        - Switch between views                │
│   Q          - Quit application                    │
│   R          - Refresh current view data           │
│   ?          - Show this help                      │
│                                                     │
│ Session View:                                      │
│   ↑/↓        - Navigate session list               │
│   Enter      - Show session details                │
│   N          - Create new session                  │
│   D          - Open debug view for selected        │
│   C          - Close selected session              │
│   L          - Show logs for selected session      │
│   B          - Run benchmark on selected           │
│                                                     │
│ Session Debug View:                                │
│   ESC        - Return to session list              │
│   ↑/↓        - Scroll sections                     │
│                                                     │
│ Channel View:                                      │
│   ↑/↓        - Navigate channel list               │
│   F          - Change node filter                  │
│                                                     │
│ Session Creation Dialog:                           │
│   Tab        - Move between fields                 │
│   ↑/↓        - Change selection                    │
│   Enter      - Create session                      │
│   ESC        - Cancel and close                    │
│                                                     │
│ Quick Start:                                       │
│   1. Ensure HOPRd nodes are running                │
│   2. Configure nodes in config.toml                │
│   3. Launch: hose --config config.toml             │
│   4. Press 'N' to create your first session        │
│   5. Press 'D' to debug session behavior           │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

FEATURES:
- Comprehensive keyboard shortcut reference
- Grouped by view (Global, Session View, etc.)
- Quick start guide at bottom
- Scrollable if content is long
- Open with '?' key or F1

INTERACTION:
- Up/Down: scroll
- ESC or ?: close help
- Tab: switch to other views

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

✓ Explain each view's purpose
✓ Show quick start instructions
✓ Press '?' or F1 to open
✓ Grouped by context
✓ Scrollable if needed

title: Config View
description:
Implement configuration display view (read-only).

VIEW LAYOUT:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Configuration                                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ HOPRd Nodes:                                       │
│   entry-node (Entry)                               │
│     Endpoint: http://localhost:3001                │
│     Auth: ✓ Token configured                      │
│   relay-node-1 (Relay)                            │
│     Endpoint: http://localhost:3002                │
│   exit-node (Exit)                                │
│     Endpoint: http://localhost:3003                │
│                                                     │
│ Session Defaults:                                  │
│   Protocol: TCP                                    │
│   Timeout: 5000ms                                  │

│   Buffer Size: 8192 bytes                          │
│                                                     │
│ Benchmark Settings:                                │

│   Concurrency: 10                                  │
│                                                     │

│   Theme: dark                                      │

INTERACTION:

title: Channel View
description:
Implement channel list view.

VIEW LAYOUT:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Channels from: [All Nodes ▼]                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ID       │ Source   │ Destination │ Status │ Balance   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 0x8f3a.. │ entry    │ relay1      │ OPEN   │ 0.5 HOPR  │
│ 0x2b4c.. │ relay1   │ relay2      │ OPEN   │ 1.2 HOPR  │
│ 0x9e1f.. │ relay2   │ exit        │ OPEN   │ 0.8 HOPR  │
│ 0x3d2a.. │ entry    │ exit        │ CLOSED │ 0.0 HOPR  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

FEATURES:
- Display table of channels from configured nodes
- Filter by node (dropdown: All Nodes, entry-node, relay1, etc.)
- Show: channel ID (short), source node, destination node, status, balance
- Arrow keys to navigate
- Refresh data from API on view enter and on 'R' key
- Color coding:
  - Green: OPEN status
  - Red: CLOSED status
  - Yellow: PendingToClose status

DATA SOURCE:
Query HOPRd API for channels:
- GET /api/v3/channels (all channels from node)
- Fetch from each configured node in parallel
- Aggregate results

REFRESH BEHAVIOR:
- Auto-refresh every 5 seconds (slower than sessions)
- Manual refresh with 'R' key
- Show "Refreshing..." indicator

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ Display table of all channels
✓ Show ID, source, destination, status, balance
✓ Filter by node (dropdown)
✓ Refresh data from API
✓ Color-coded by status
✓ Arrow key navigation

title: Example Configurations
description:
Create example configuration files.

EXAMPLE CONFIGS:

1. config.example.toml (local 4-node setup):
```toml
# HOPR Session Debugger Configuration
# This example shows a local 4-node setup for development

[hoprd]
[[hoprd.nodes]]
name = "entry-node"
api_endpoint = "http://localhost:3001"
api_token = "your-token-here"  # Get from hoprd-admin
role = "Entry"
peer_id = "16Uiu2..."  # Optional

[[hoprd.nodes]]
name = "relay-node-1"
api_endpoint = "http://localhost:3002"
api_token = "relay-token-1"
role = "Relay"

[[hoprd.nodes]]
name = "relay-node-2"
api_endpoint = "http://localhost:3003"
api_token = "relay-token-2"
role = "Relay"

[[hoprd.nodes]]
name = "exit-node"
api_endpoint = "http://localhost:3004"
api_token = "exit-token"
role = "Exit"

[sessions]
default_protocol = "TCP"
timeout_ms = 5000
max_retries = 3
buffer_size = 8192

[benchmark]
iterations = 100
payload_sizes = [64, 512, 1024, 4096, 8192]
concurrency = 10

[tui]
refresh_rate_ms = 500
theme = "dark"
log_level = "info"
```

2. config.remote.toml (remote nodes):
```toml
# Remote node configuration
# Connect to HOPRd nodes running on different machines

[hoprd]
[[hoprd.nodes]]
name = "entry-node"
api_endpoint = "https://entry.hopr.example.com:3001"
api_token = "prod-token-1"
role = "Entry"

[[hoprd.nodes]]
name = "exit-node"
api_endpoint = "https://exit.hopr.example.com:3001"
api_token = "prod-token-2"
role = "Exit"

[sessions]
default_protocol = "TCP"
timeout_ms = 10000  # Higher timeout for remote
max_retries = 5
buffer_size = 8192

[benchmark]
iterations = 50  # Fewer iterations for remote
payload_sizes = [512, 1024, 4096]
concurrency = 5

[tui]
refresh_rate_ms = 1000  # Slower refresh for remote
theme = "dark"
log_level = "info"
```

3. config.minimal.toml (simplest config):
```toml
# Minimal configuration for 0-hop direct connections

[hoprd]
[[hoprd.nodes]]
name = "local-node"
api_endpoint = "http://localhost:3001"
api_token = "token"
role = "Entry"

[[hoprd.nodes]]
name = "peer-node"
api_endpoint = "http://localhost:3002"
role = "Exit"

[sessions]
default_protocol = "TCP"
timeout_ms = 5000
max_retries = 3
buffer_size = 8192

[benchmark]
iterations = 100
payload_sizes = [1024]
concurrency = 1

[tui]
refresh_rate_ms = 500
theme = "dark"
log_level = "info"
```

DOCUMENTATION IN EACH FILE:
- Comment explaining purpose
- Comment on each field (what it does)
- Valid value ranges
- Default values (if not specified)
- Links to docs for more info

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
✓ config.example.toml (local 4-node)
✓ config.remote.toml (remote nodes)
✓ config.minimal.toml (simplest setup)
✓ All examples are valid TOML
✓ All examples are well-documented with comments
✓ Examples cover common use cases


DOCUMENTATION REQUIREMENT:
Upon completion, you MUST update all relevant documentation:
- README.md: Update if user-facing behavior or installation changes
- AGENTS.md: Update if new modules, APIs, or architecture changes
- Architecture doc: Update if system design or data flow changes
Include these updates in the same commit/PR as the task completion.
